prompt: Design a curriculum to teach a multilingual base model (Qwen2-7B) to consistently respond in the language the user wrote in - resisting drift to English on long responses about technical topics. Cover at least 6 languages (en, es, fr, de, ja, zh). Output stages with (dataset, success criterion).
reference_solution:
Curriculum: teach Qwen2-7B language-consistency under technical-topic load.

Stage 1 - Per-language SFT baseline.
  Dataset: 80k instruction-following examples, balanced 10k per language (en/es/fr/de/ja/zh + ko + pt to even buckets). Source: Open-Hermes-2.5 + Aya + cohere-aya-collection filtered + machine-translated stratifying.
  Success: per-language win-rate >= 60% vs base model on a 500-example AlpacaEval-style harness.

Stage 2 - Technical vocabulary in each language.
  Dataset: 20k (technical-prompt, technical-response) pairs per language covering ML, software, math, physics. Source: machine-translated from English technical corpora with human spot-check for native-speaker terminology.
  Success: terminology-correctness >= 80% on a 100-example native-rater spot-check per language.

Stage 3 - Language identification + sticking.
  Dataset: 30k (multilingual-prompt, same-language-response) pairs with response lengths 500-2000 tokens. fastText langid loss on the response is added as a constrained-decoding signal during data prep to filter drifters.
  Success: 95% language-match (fastText langid agreement between prompt and response) on a 1k held-out across all 6 target languages.

Stage 4 - Code-switching robustness.
  Dataset: 10k examples where the prompt contains short English code identifiers / library names embedded in a non-English request. Response must explain in the prompt's language but keep code identifiers in English.
  Success: 90% language-match on prose paragraphs; code-identifier-preservation >= 99%.

Stage 5 - Negative drift correction.
  Dataset: 5k DPO pairs (in-language, drift-to-english) generated by intentionally degrading stage 3 outputs.
  Success: implicit-reward margin >= 1.5; downstream language-match up by 2+ pts vs stage 4 baseline.

Stage 6 - Held-out eval.
  Dataset: 200 prompts per language (1200 total) sampled from real-world web logs in each language, all on technical topics.
  Success: per-language language-match >= 92% across all 6 target languages.
